page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place the ecs.c7t.4xlarge in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1598.0 and a multi-core score of 9553.0. In cryptography and compression tests, the instance registers a PassMark CPU encryption score of 14642.3 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 84.5 GB/sec. For database workloads, it handles 15.82 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 7532.07 on PassMark database operations.

The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and SHA_NI, which assist in vector calculations and cryptographic operations. In LLM inference benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong performance for text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 263.14 tokens per second, while prompt processing for the same model registers at 1299.46 tokens per second. For medium 7B models, text generation drops to 12.52 tokens per second.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and dedicated physical cores, the ecs.c7t.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local SSD storage. It is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, medium-scale database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, it provides a 2.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9553.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 21125.85. It exhibits strong performance in text generation for small LLM models, reaching 263.14 tokens per second. Supported by AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for dedicated CPU-bound tasks. It is best suited for software compilation, web serving, database operations, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for CPU-intensive workloads and web serving.
